subject: refactor(theme): implement proper light/dark mode, remove all hardcoded colors
body:
Replace ~55 hardcoded color values across 19 files with theme-derived tokens.
Ensures consistent appearance in both light and dark modes with proper contrast.

Key changes:
- Fix Themed.tsx: was hardcoded to light-only, now uses useColorScheme
- Remove static Theme export from Colors.ts (was always DarkTheme)
- Root layout now references LightTheme/DarkTheme constants
- +html.tsx CSS uses theme constants via template interpolation
- All #FFFFFF on brand backgrounds → textOnPrimary token
- FuelMapWeb: map style reactive to system color scheme (dark-v11/light-v11)
- MapErrorBoundary: class component uses Appearance API for theme detection
- Toast: uses AppColors.price.fresh/LightTheme.error instead of raw hex
- Switch track color uses t.border instead of hardcoded #D1D5DB
- All components use useAppTheme() hook for dynamic color values

Remaining #000 values are shadowColor — standard for both modes.

Tags: theme, dark-mode, light-mode, colors, accessibility, contrast
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: theme-system
Semver: minor
